description: Structured attribution decisions for the README Acknowledgments section. The model proposes zero or more entries; each entry is either a new one-liner, a promotion of an existing one-liner to a multi-bullet entry, or a new bullet appended to an existing multi-bullet entry.

should_update.description: True if at least one entry warrants an Acknowledgments-section change. False if the PR has no externally-attributable contribution (e.g. solo PR by the maintainer with no substantive issue/PR commenter input).

reasoning.description: Short explanation of the decision. Cite which contributors were considered and why each was included or excluded.

action.description: add = new one-line entry at the end of the list; promote = existing one-line entry becomes multi-bullet (preserves the original line as the first bullet, adds the new line as the second); append = new bullet on an existing multi-bullet entry.

username.description: GitHub login (no leading @, no slashes, no spaces). Validated against the GitHub username pattern.

description.description: For action=add: the body of the new one-liner (the part after the linked username, e.g. 'for fixing the launcher cleanup race'). For action=promote: the new bullet to append (the original line stays as the first bullet automatically). For action=append: the new bullet text. Reference PR/issue numbers in parentheses at the end where relevant, e.g. '(#562)'.
